title: neuontobotrys choiquensis
intro: Neuontobotrys choiquensis, also known as Chilean boxwood or Quebracho colorado, is a flowering plant species native to the Andes Mountains in South America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems. The medicinal properties of Neuontobotrys choiquensis include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing pain and inflammation, and fighting off infections. Herbal preparations of Neuontobotrys choiquensis can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water, decocting the roots, or using the plant's extracts in various forms, making it a versatile and valuable medicinal plant.
